description: DIY homeowner sequence. Technical education over 45 days with a later Angi Electrical CTA for the work that is usually worth hiring out.

subject_lines: Your {{ home.tonnage }}-ton sizing — and the honest DIY HVAC playbook
DIY HVAC: what is realistic and what is not
sequence.preheader: The honest version: some HVAC work is realistic DIY, and some is better hired out.

subject_lines: Pulling your own HVAC permit: the homeowner walkthrough
State permits for DIY HVAC: how it works
sequence.preheader: Permits are less mysterious when you know what the inspector is checking.

subject_lines: The DIY HVAC toolkit: what you actually need
Tools for DIY HVAC: the practical list
sequence.preheader: Buy the tools that match the project. Skip the ones that only make sense for daily pro use.

subject_lines: Pre-charged mini-splits: the realistic DIY path
Single-zone, multi-zone, and the DIY mini-split tradeoffs
sequence.preheader: Pre-charged line sets are why DIY HVAC is possible for more homeowners now.

subject_lines: When to call a pro: 3 HVAC tasks not worth DIY-ing
The 3 parts of a DIY HVAC install to hire out
sequence.preheader: Your time and your insurance both have a price. These are the tasks to price carefully.

subject_lines: Tax-credit paperwork for a DIY heat-pump install
DIY HVAC paperwork: receipts, certification statements, and install dates
sequence.preheader: Self-installs can still need serious paperwork. Keep the records before tax season.

personalization_variables.home.sqft: number — square footage from the load calc
personalization_variables.location.climate_region: string — Hot / Warm / Mixed / Cool / Cold

notes: Third-party product/vendor names from the draft are intentionally generalized for NTQ public-copy rules.
